title: ribes leptanthum subsp. lasianthum
intro: Ribes leptanthum subsp. lasianthum, also known as the Sierra gooseberry, is a species of flowering plant in the currant family (Grossulariaceae) native to western North America. The Sierra gooseberry has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and flavonoids. The plant's health benefits include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The Sierra gooseberry can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues.
